hooks.prompt:
FIRST: check the file path. If the file path does NOT contain /scenes/ or \scenes\ — return {"ok": true} immediately. Do NOT inspect the content. Do NOT check formatting. Just return ok and stop.

Only if the file IS inside scenes/, proceed with checks.

Determine project type from the file content ONLY: if the first heading starts with '# Scene' or '# Сцена' — it is fiction. If it starts with '# Block' or '# Блок' — it is documentary. If neither — assume fiction. Do NOT try to read CLAUDE.md or any other file. Do NOT fail because project type is unknown.

Check the content that was just written:

[ONE SLUG PER FILE — FICTION] Count lines starting with INT. or EXT. or ИНТ. or НАТ. (not inside a heading or table). If more than one slug line is found — return {"ok": false, "reason": "Multiple slug lines detected. Split this file now: each slug becomes its own scene file. Renumber subsequent scenes to accommodate the new files. Do not ask for confirmation."} immediately, no further checks.

[FORMAT — FICTION] Fountain format. ZERO bold anywhere. No double asterisks anywhere in the file. Slug lines = plain uppercase (ИНТ./НАТ./INT./EXT.). Character names = plain uppercase (ТОЛЕПБЕРГЕН, not **ТОЛЕПБЕРГЕН**). If you see double asterisks in the file — that is the ONLY error to flag. Spacing: exactly one blank line between blocks; never two blank lines before a character name; zero blank lines between name and dialogue.

[FORMAT — DOCUMENTARY] Heading starts with '# Block' or '# Блок'. Table with | VIDEO | AUDIO | columns.

[AI PATTERNS] Flag if present: emotional placeholders (looks thoughtfully, sighs meaningfully), dialogue that explains feelings (I am angry because...), exactly 3 adjectives in a row, filler phrases (it is important to note, plays a vital role).

[ACTION BLOCKS — FICTION] Each beat = its own paragraph with a blank line between. Flag if two or more distinct actions are merged into one paragraph.

[VALUE SHIFT — FICTION] Does something change? Entry state vs exit state on any axis: safety/danger, hope/despair, connection/isolation, truth/lie, power/powerlessness. If the character ends in the same state they entered — flag it.

[PRODUCTIVE BLOCK — DOCUMENTARY] Does the block reveal new information, complicate, or answer something? If it only repeats what the audience already knows — flag it.

Return {"ok": true} if no issues. Return {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue"} if issues found. One reason only — the most critical.
